title: CBP Agents Kill U.S. Citizen Alex Pretti in Minneapolis; Video Contradicts DHS Account
excerpt: Border Patrol agents shot and killed 37-year-old ICU nurse Alex Pretti during Operation Metro Surge in Minneapolis. Video reviewed by multiple news organizations appeared to contradict DHS claims that Pretti had 'brandished' a weapon.
content:
On January 24, 2026, two U.S. Customs and Border Protection officers fatally shot Alex Jeffrey Pretti, a 37-year-old VA intensive care nurse and U.S. citizen, near the intersection of 26th Street and Nicollet Avenue in Minneapolis during Operation Metro Surge.

DHS Secretary Kristi Noem described Pretti as a domestic terrorist who arrived to 'inflict maximum damage.' Video verified by ABC News, Reuters, BBC, and The Wall Street Journal showed Pretti holding only a phone before being shoved, pepper-sprayed, and tackled by at least six agents before shots were fired. At no point in the initial DHS report did officials state Pretti attempted to reach for his weapon.

  • 10 shots were fired in less than five seconds, according to forensic audio analysis.
  • An internal CBP review found the agent who took Pretti's firearm did so before shots were fired — contradicting the official 'he had a gun' justification.
  • Federal investigators physically blocked Minnesota Bureau of Criminal Apprehension agents from the shooting scene.
  • The DOJ Civil Rights Division opened an investigation on January 30, 2026.
  • ProPublica identified the two shooters as Border Patrol Agent Jesus Ochoa, 43, and CBP Officer Raymundo Gutierrez, 35, both assigned to Operation Metro Surge.
'What the videos depict is that this guy did not walk up to anybody from CBP in a threatening manner.' — Former Acting DHS Undersecretary for Intelligence John Cohen, ABC News contributor
mediaPlaceholder: Verified bystander video showing Pretti being tackled by multiple agents moments before fatal shooting
imageAlt: CBP Agents Kill U.S. Citizen Alex Pretti in Minneapolis; Video Contradicts DHS Account
